name: Carmen
image_alt: Long tapered red Italian frying peppers on the plant
description: An All-America Selections winner, Carmen produces long, tapered Italian-style frying peppers that ripen from green to a brilliant red. One of the sweetest and most reliable sweet peppers for home gardens.
history: Introduced by Harris Seeds in 2006 after winning AAS honors. Bred specifically to combine the classic Italian frying pepper shape with improved disease resistance and productivity for home gardeners.

good.reason: Loosens soil and doesn't compete heavily for nutrients
Similar cultural needs, can be interplanted

bad.reason: Attract different pests, compete for space

seasonal_quirks: Needs consistent heat to set and ripen well. In cool summers, fruit stays green longer — flavor is still good but sweetness fully develops only when red. Very productive once temperatures stabilize above 70°F nights.
harvest_cues: Pick green for a firmer, slightly bitter flavor, or wait for full red for maximum sweetness. The transition from green to red takes 2–3 weeks. Fruit reaches 6–8 inches when mature.
variety_notes: One of the top-performing sweet peppers in university trials. Thick walls, very few seeds, and a cavity that's perfect for stuffing or roasting whole over a flame.
preservation: Roast and freeze in strips. Excellent pickled or packed in oil after roasting.
